name: Deep Blue
parameter_count: Not applicable — hardware-based evaluation function with 480 custom chess chips
training_approach: Hand-tuned evaluation function with grandmaster consultation, alpha-beta pruning search, and specialized VLSI chess processors
key_innovations: First computer system to defeat a reigning world chess champion under tournament conditions
Evaluated 200 million positions per second using custom chess hardware
Combined brute-force search depth with hand-crafted chess knowledge
Demonstrated that specialized hardware could solve problems previously considered exclusive to human intelligence
lineage_path: Shannon Chess Programming -> Minimax -> Alpha-Beta Pruning -> Deep Blue
cultural_impact: Deep Blue's 1997 victory over Garry Kasparov was one of the most widely covered AI events in history, appearing on front pages worldwide. It became a cultural touchstone for AI capability and raised fundamental questions about machine intelligence. Kasparov's accusation of cheating reflected the deep psychological impact of a machine surpassing human expertise in a domain considered to require deep intelligence.
